messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "prelet-pipeline-lockout",
 "title": "The growth the CAGR promises was electrically specified before launch day — Year 1 can only sell into the installed base",
 "narrative": "Nine percent CAGR reads as a tailwind: new halls, new racks, new PDU line items every quarter of Year 1. But new-build DC electrical procurement is locked at design freeze, and with long-lead power equipment (transformers, switchgear) now booked years out, operators are locking entire electrical BOMs — PDUs riding along — far earlier than the 12–24-month window the ledger's own crossCheck uses to justify its ceiling. Every megawatt that energizes during the venture's first 12 months had its rack-power vendor chosen while the venture was still pre-launch. That leaves Year-1 demand confined to retrofit, refresh and expansion churn in the installed base — a materially smaller and slower pool than 'the market' — while the model applies its 1% to the whole SAM as if new-build spend were reachable on the venture's clock.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "New-build electrical BOMs for capacity energizing in the venture's Year 1 were specified at design freeze, before the venture existed as a qualified vendor; the ledger's own crossCheck concedes gear is 'locked 12–24mo before fit-out' and current long-lead conditions push lock-in earlier still",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "profile:Stage"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The market's growth is concentrated in exactly this pre-committed new-build pipeline (the substance of the 9% CAGR), so the contestable Year-1 pool is the installed base's refresh cycle — and what standalone PDU procurement evidence exists shows small, sometimes sole-sourced lots with 1–2 bidders, not open bake-offs",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:shape.cagr",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c12"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Generic obtainable-share benchmarks (1% Year 1) assume the full SAM is time-reachable; no DC-hardware-specific benchmark exists that discounts for procurement lock-in, so the model has no mechanism to encode it",
   "refs": [
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c0",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c2"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "obtainableFactor applied to the full SAM double-counts pre-committed new-build spend as reachable; the truly contestable Year-1 share sits at or below the band floor",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The founder's plan treats the CAGR as an accelerant and 1% as conservative precisely because the market is growing — 'we only need a tiny slice of a rising tide.' The trap is that the rising portion of the tide is the least reachable portion on a 12-month clock; the model has no node that separates committed-pipeline demand from contestable demand, so nothing in the ledger flags it.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Documented cases of CE new-build data centers running competitive rack-PDU selection within 6 months of fit-out (i.e., inside the venture's selling window), or an operator confirming PDUs are procured late as a commodity line decoupled from the electrical design freeze.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.5,
  "rationale": "Early electrical lock-in for new builds is well-documented industry behavior and the ledger's own crossCheck relies on a 12–24-month version of it; the offsetting uncertainty is how much PDU spend is genuinely late-cycle commodity purchasing versus BOM-committed — unquantified in every retrieved source.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.005,
   "note": "Year-1 capture limited to installed-base retrofit/refresh; new-build pipeline pre-committed before launch"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Stage at which rack-PDU vendor is fixed in CE fit-out tenders (bundled at design freeze vs late separate lot)",
   "where": "TED tender documents for DE/NL data-center fit-outs; fit-out contractors' bid packages",
   "threshold": "PDU line bundled into general-electrical packages in ≥3 of 4 sampled tenders",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Pre-let percentages and long-lead procurement commentary for CE capacity additions",
   "where": "Operator and hyperscaler capex calls (Equinix, Digital Realty quarterly); Vertiv/Schneider backlog commentary",
   "threshold": "Pre-let >80% with electrical packages cited as booked >18 months out",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Map the 2026–27 CE new-build pipeline by procurement stage (design freeze reached? electrical package awarded?) to quantify the actually-contestable Year-1 pool",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Separates committed from contestable SAM — the distinction the funnel lacks"
  },
  {
   "action": "Target the installed-base refresh cycle explicitly: build the launch offer around drop-in replacement and retrofit economics (metering upgrades, DCIM integration) rather than new-build spec wins",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "execution-window",
 "riskId": "risk.execution-window.prelet-pipeline-lockout"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

But new-build DC electrical procurement is locked at design freeze, and with long-lead power equipment (transformers, switchgear) now booked years out, operators are locking entire electrical BOMs — PDUs riding along — far earlier than the 12–24-month window the ledger's own crossCheck uses to justify its ceiling. Every megawatt that energizes during the venture's first 12 months had its rack-power vendor chosen while the venture was still pre-launch. electrical package awarded?) to quantify the actually-contestable Year-1 pool","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Separates committed from contestable SAM — the distinction the funnel lacks"},{"action":"Target the installed-base refresh cycle explicitly: build the launch offer around drop-in replacement and retrofit economics (metering upgrades, DCIM integration) rather than new-build spec wins","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
